source_pr.title: planner, executor: fix index merge partial table scan schema
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/23919

Problem Summary: 

```sql
create table t (a int, b int, index(a), index(b)) partition by hash (a) partitions 2;
insert into t values (1, 5);
select /*+ use_index_merge( t ) */ * from t where a in (3) or b in (5) order by a;
```

```console
ERROR 1105 (HY000): runtime error: index out of range [2] with length 2
```

The explain result:
```
+--------------------------------------+---------+-----------+-----------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+
| id                                   | estRows | task      | access object                     | operator info                               |
+--------------------------------------+---------+-----------+-----------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+
| Sort_9                               | 39.98   | root      |                                   | test.t.a                                    |
| â””â”€PartitionUnion_12                  | 39.98   | root      |                                   |                                             |
|   â”śâ”€IndexMerge_16                    | 19.99   | root      |                                   |                                             |
|   â”‚ â”śâ”€IndexRangeScan_13(Build)       | 10.00   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p0, index:a(a) | range:[3,3], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
|   â”‚ â”śâ”€IndexRangeScan_14(Build)       | 10.00   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p0, index:b(b) | range:[5,5], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
|   â”‚ â””â”€TableRowIDScan_15(Probe)       | 19.99   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p0             | keep order:false, stats:pseudo              |
|   â””â”€IndexMerge_20                    | 19.99   | root      |                                   |                                             |
|     â”śâ”€IndexRangeScan_17(Build)       | 10.00   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p1, index:a(a) | range:[3,3], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
|     â”śâ”€IndexRangeScan_18(Build)       | 10.00   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p1, index:b(b) | range:[5,5], keep order:false, stats:pseudo |
|     â””â”€TableRowIDScan_19(Probe)       | 19.99   | cop[tikv] | table:t, partition:p1             | keep order:false, stats:pseudo              |
+--------------------------------------+---------+-----------+-----------------------------------+---------------------------------------------+
```

`SortExec.Next()` only requires 2 columns(`a` and `b`), but the row fetched from the underlying `UnionExec` has 3(`a`, `b` and `_tidb_rowid`). This cause `Chunk.AppendPartialRow` panic because the slice out-of-range error.

The request chunk for `UnionExec.Next` should have only 2 columns, but `req.SwapColumns(result.chk)` change it into a 3-columns chunk. This chunk comes from `IndexMergeReaderExecutor`, which has a 3-columns schema.

`IndexMergeReaderExecutor` inherits the schema from `PhysicalIndexMergeReader`, which inherits from the `PhysicalTableScan` built by `buildIndexMergeTableScan`:

https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/008b91b12b0780539911da9f533d42114a213d87/planner/core/find_best_task.go#L903-L914

It appends the `_tidb_rowid` to the schema of `DataSource` to help `HandleCols` resolve its indices.

The `HandleCols` is used to fetch handles in both `TableRangeScan` and `IndexRangeScan`. 
- For the `TableRangeScan`, the current schema is from `DataSource` plus additional common handle columns. This is unresonable because only the handle columns are needed.
- For the `IndexRangeScan`, the current schema is always indexed columns plus full common handle columns. `ResolveIndices` is useless in `IndexRangeScan` because the `expression.Column.Index` is never used.


### What is changed and how it works?

How it Works: 

This PR makes the schema of `IndexMerge` consistent with the upper operator, by avoid changing `DataSource` schema directly. It also fixes the issue in https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/23933#pullrequestreview-633357194.

What is changed:

- Change the schema of `TableRangeScan`(as a child of `IndexMerge` operator) to the one that only contains handle columns.
- Resolve the `HandleCols` in `IndexMergeReaderExecutor` against the schema that only contains the handle columns.
- Remove unnecessary projection.
- Add test for #23919 and `clustered index/_tidb_rowid/PkIsHandle` for index merge.

### Related changes

 NA

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

Side effects

NA

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Fix an issue that sorting on index-merge results in partition union reports 'index out of range'.

symptoms: const queryTemplate = "select /*+ use_index_merge(t) */ %s from t where %s order by a, b;" (planner/core/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: const queryTemplate = "select /*+ use_index_merge(t) */ %s from t where %s order by a, b;" (planner/core/integration_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: const queryTemplate = "select /*+ use_index_merge(t) */ %s from t where %s order by a, b;" (planner/core/integration_test.go).
fix_pattern: const queryTemplate = "select /*+ use_index_merge(t) */ %s from t where %s order by a, b;" (planner/core/integration_test.go)

evidence: evidence: const queryTemplate = "select /*+ use_index_merge(t) */ %s from t where %s order by a, b;" (planner/core/integration_test.go)

files: executor/executor.go, executor/index_merge_reader.go, planner/core/exhaust_physical_plans.go, planner/core/find_best_task.go, planner/core/handle_cols.go, planner/core/integration_test.go, planner/core/task.go, planner/core/testdata/integration_suite_out.json, table/tables/index.go
